title: Chapter 6 Keeping Your Electrical System in Tune

answers.question: For which types of vehicles are ignition tune-ups generally no longer needed annually?
options: Vehicles that are frequently driven off-road
justification: “U nlike older vehicles, which needed ignition tune-ups at least once a year, most vehicles on the road today have electronic ignition systems that are built to go for years without the need for them.”

answers.question: What is a typical component of a modern tune-up?
justification: In case you’re wondering what a modern tune-up includes, it generally involves doing an electronic diagnostic check, changing spark plugs, replacing air and fuel filters, and checking fluid levels.

answers.question: Why should scheduled tune-ups be performed?
options: To maintain vehicle warranty or leasing agreements
To improve the vehicle’s resale value only
answers.answer: To maintain vehicle warranty or leasing agreements
justification: And remember, if you fail to get a scheduled tune-up you could violate your vehicle’s warranty or leas-ing agreement.

answers.question: Which of the following electrical system tasks are generally suitable for a vehicle owner to perform?
justification: There are several jobs involving the electrical system that you can deal with yourself...You also can troubleshoot and replace fuses...

answers.question: What is the recommended tune-up interval for most older vehicles with non-electronic ignitions?
justification:
Most older vehicles with
non-electronic ignitions should be tuned every 10,000 to 12,000 miles or
every year, whichever comes first.

answers.question: Which of the following systems is explicitly advised against attempting to repair without professional assistance?
justification:
Don’t attempt to work on your air conditioner under any circumstances. It con- 
contains refrigerant under pressure that can blind you if it escapes. For service 
or repairs, head to the dealership or to an automotive air-conditioning 
specialist.

answers.question: According to the text, what might contribute to a vehicle stalling?
justification: The car stalls a lot. The spark plugs may be fouled or worn, the gap between the spark plug electrodes may need adjusting, or an elec-\ntronic sensing device may need to be adjusted.

answers.question: What is suggested as a benefit of periodically checking a vehicle's efficiency, even if the owner’s manual doesn't recommend frequent tune-ups?
options: To extend the lifespan of the engine
justification:
Refer to your owner’s manual for recommended tune-up intervals, but be
aware that even if it says that the vehicle doesn’t require scheduled tune-ups
very often, it’s in your best interest to check periodically that your vehicle’s
working at peak efficiency.

answers.question: What is one possible cause for a vehicle experiencing difficulty starting?
answers.justification: The car gets harder to start. The problem can be in the starting system (for example, a weak battery), in the fuel system (for example, a weak fuel pump), in the ignition system, or can be due to an electronic compo-nent, such as the electronic control unit (ECU).

answers.question: What is a potential indicator that a vehicle needs a tune-up?
justification: The engine is running roughly when idling or when you accelerate. Chances are the vehicle needs a tune-up.

answers.question: What is a key difference between 30,000-mile spark plugs and those with platinum tips?
options: 30,000-mile plugs are better suited for V-type engines.
Platinum-tipped plugs can sometimes last up to 100,000 miles.
Platinum-tipped plugs typically require less frequent replacement.
answers.answer: Platinum-tipped plugs can sometimes last up to 100,000 miles.
justification: You may have 30,000-mile plugs, or if the plugs have platinum tips, they may be good for up to 100,000 miles, although some professionals recommend replacement every 60,000 miles to avoid damage to the engine.

answers.question: What component do spark plug wires connect to?
answers.justification: These spark-plug wires run from the ignition coil (or the distributor on older vehicles) to the spark plugs.

answers.question: What is a characteristic of engines with a V-type configuration?
options: They always use platinum-tipped spark plugs.
They typically have larger engine blocks.
They have spark plug wires on both sides.
They have spark plug wires on one side.
answers.answer: They have spark plug wires on both sides.
justification: on both sides if you have a V-type engine or on one side if you have a 4- or 6-cylinder in-line engine

answers.question: What is the most significant potential consequence of cross-threading a spark plug?
options: Need for a multi-thousand dollar repair
answers.answer: Need for a multi-thousand dollar repair
justification: Both are bad news. You could end up needing a multi-thousand dollar repair.

answers.question: Why might removing a metal shield covering the engine be problematic?
options: It limits access to the spark plugs.
justification: Still other engines conceal the plugs beneath a metal shield that covers\n the engine. Removing this shield can require disconnecting cables that\n can seriously affect the ECU. That’s why the shield is there!

answers.question: What is the recommended quantity of spark plugs to purchase?
justification: Buy one for each cylinder in your engine.

answers.question: Why should you cover the fender while working?
options: To keep it clean from grease
answers.justification:
“An old blanket, mattress pad, or padded car protector to place over
the fender where you’ll be working to protect it from scratches

answers.question: What is suggested as an alternative to commercial car protectors?
options: Using a new set of spark plugs
Using an old blanket or mattress pad
answers.answer: Using an old blanket or mattress pad
justification:
Commercial car protectors often come with handy pockets that hold
tools and little parts while you work. You can make such a pocket your-
s \self by pinning up the bottom edge of your folded blanket or pad.

answers.question: According to the material, why shouldn't you change just a few spark plugs?
options: It is not possible to change just a few plugs
It will require more specialized tools
It can negatively affect engine performance
It is more difficult to change just a few plugs
answers.answer: It can negatively affect engine performance
justification: Never change just a few plugs; it’s all or nothing for even engine perfor-mance.

answers.question: What is a suggested purpose of having an extra spark plug?
options: To use during your next engine overhaul
To provide a backup for your work light
To have in case of damage or defects
answers.answer: To have in case of damage or defects
justification: If you’re feeling especially wealthy, buy an extra plug in case you get home and find that one of them is defective, or in case you acciden- tally ruin one by dropping and cracking it or by cross-threading it when you install it.

answers.question: What is one reason to apply silicone lubricant to spark plug boots?
options: To extend the life of the engine.
To prevent them from sticking to the spark plug.
answers.answer: to prevent them from sticking to the porcelain
justification: silicone lubricant to them to prevent them from sticking to the porcelain

answers.question: What tool is used to adjust the gap between a spark plug's electrodes?
justification: A wire or taper feeler gauge: You use this to gap spark plugs.

answers.question: Where can you typically find spark plug specifications for a vehicle?
options: Owner's manual or a 'Tune-Up Specification Guide'
answers.answer: Owner's manual or a 'Tune-Up Specification Guide'
justification: Your owner’s manual may have specifications for buying and gapping the spark plugs on your vehicle. If you don’t have an owner’s manual, or if yours lacks the necessary information, you can find the correct spark plugs and spark plug gap in a general “Tune-Up Specification Guide”

answers.question: Which of the following is NOT a piece of information needed to obtain spark plug specifications?
justification: These identifi...paint it has, and so on, but don’t worry about that information right now.

answers.question: What does engine displacement refer to?
options: The presence of air conditioning in a vehicle.
The number of barrels in a carburetor.
The amount of room in each cylinder when the piston is at its lowest point.
The type of fuel system used in an engine.
answers.answer: The amount of room in each cylinder when the piston is at its lowest point.
justification: The engine displacement: How much room there is in each cylinder when the piston is at its lowest point?

answers.question: Modern vehicles typically list engine displacement in which units?
options: A combination of barrels and liters.
justification: Modern vehicles are usually listed in liters (1.8, 2.3, 5.9) or cubic centimeters (2200, 3400, 3800).

answers.question: Which of the following best describes a key difference between older and newer vehicles regarding engine displacement?
options: Older vehicles generally used liters, while newer vehicles used cubic inches.
Older vehicles generally used cubic inches, while newer vehicles use liters or cubic centimeters.
Both older and newer vehicles exclusively use cubic inches.
Both older and newer vehicles exclusively use liters.
answers.answer: Older vehicles generally used cubic inches, while newer vehicles use liters or cubic centimeters.
justification: The displacement of engines on older vehicles may be listed in cubic inches, such as 302, 350, 454, and so on. Modern vehicles are usually listed in liters (1.8, 2.3, 5.9) or cubic centimeters (2200, 3400, 3800).

answers.question: What is one potential distinction between fuel systems in older engines?
options: The number of barrels in the carburetor.
The presence or absence of fuel injection.
The type of spark plug used.
The resistance of the camshaft position sensor.
answers.answer: The number of barrels in the carburetor.
answers.justification: Carburetors, on the other hand, were distinguished by how many “barrels” they had.

answers.question: According to the material, what is the best approach when purchasing spark plugs?
options: Asking for a plug by a general description of your vehicle.
Relying solely on a salesperson's recommendation.
Consulting a spec sheet based on your vehicle's make, model, and year.
Purchasing the most expensive spark plugs available.
answers.answer: Consulting a spec sheet based on your vehicle's make, model, and year.
justification:
At the auto supply store, don’t just ask a salesperson which type of plug you
should buy — you have a very good chance of getting the wrong one. First
look up the specifications yourself, and then ask for the plug by number. If
you’re unsure, have a salesperson double-check it for you.

Look up your vehicle by make and model under the proper year on the spec
sheet at the store.

answers.question: What is the ‘spark plug gap’?
options: The part number for spark plugs.
The place where the spark plug wire connects to the spark plug.
The amount of space between the center and side electrodes of a spark plug.
answers.answer: The amount of space between the center and side electrodes of a spark plug.
justification: This is the amount of space that there should be between the center and side electrodes of each spark plug.

answers.question: Why should you work on one spark plug at a time?
options: To avoid confusing yourself and potentially creating a larger problem.
To show off your skills to onlookers.
answers.answer: To avoid confusing yourself and potentially creating a larger problem.
justification: One way to royally confuse yourself and turn the relatively simple task of changing spark plugs into a nightmare is to pull all your spark plugs out at one time. To keep your sanity and to avoid turning this job into an all-weekend project, work on one spark plug at a time: Remove it, inspect it, clean it, and — if it’s salvageable — gap it. Then replace it before you move on to the next spark plug in cylinder sequence order.

answers.question: What is the purpose of cleaning around the spark plug before removal?
options: To improve the spark plug's electrical conductivity.
To ensure the plug is properly tightened.
To prevent debris from entering the cylinder.
answers.answer: To prevent debris from entering the cylinder.
justification: Cleaning the area keeps loose junk from falling down the hole into the cylinder when you remove the plug.

answers.question: When removing a spark plug, what type of force is recommended?
justification: But do it in an even, controlled manner.

answers.question: What tool is specifically designed for removing spark plugs?
justification: 3. Place your spark plug socket (the big one with the rubber lining) over the spark plug; exert some pressure while turning it slightly to be sure that it’s all the way down.

answers.question: According to the text, what is suggested when a spark plug is difficult to reach?
justification: Almost every vehicle has at least one plug that’s a miserable thing to reach. If you have one, and it’s safe for you to deal with, save it for last.

answers.question: What is the recommended direction to turn a spark plug to loosen it?
justification: 5. Loosen the spark plug by turning it counterclockwise.
Every screw, nut, bolt, screw-on cap, and so on that you encounter should loosen counter- clockwise and tighten clockwise (“lefty loosey, righty tighty”).

answers.question: What is suggested if a spark plug is difficult to loosen?
justification: If it feels stuck, try a little spray lubricant.

answers.question: What is the purpose of the knob located on the back of a ratchet handle?
options: To reverse the direction of socket rotation
To secure the handle to the wrench
answers.answer: To reverse the direction of socket rotation
justification: The little knob on the back of the ratchet handle causes the ratchet to turn the socket either clockwise or counterclockwise. You can tell which way the handle will turn the plug by lis- tening to the clicks that the handle makes when you move it in one direction. If it clicks when you move it to the right, it will turn the socket counterclockwise when you move it, silently, to the left. If the clicks are audible on the leftward swing, it will move the socket clockwise on the rightward swing.

answers.question: What general principle applies to loosening most fasteners?
justification:
Every screw, nut, bolt, screw-on cap, and so on that you encounter should loosen counter-
clockwise and tighten clockwise (“lefty loosey, righty tighty”).

answers.question: What should you ensure before applying force when loosening a spark plug?
options: The ratchet is at a different angle than the plug
The socket is securely over the plug
answers.answer: The socket is securely over the plug
justification: It’s okay to exert some strength; just be sure your socket is securely over the plug and that the ratchet is at the same angle as the plug to avoid stripping the threads on the plug or in the spark plug hole in the engine head.

answers.question: What is the purpose of the gap between the center and side electrodes of a spark plug?
options: To regulate the flow of exhaust gases
To insulate the plug from the engine block
To provide a secure connection to the engine’s wiring harness
To allow the spark to jump and ignite the fuel/air mixture
answers.answer: To allow the spark to jump and ignite the fuel/air mixture
justification: The spark comes up the center of the plug and jumps the gap between these two electrodes.

answers.question: What should be done before proceeding to the next spark plug after it has been removed?
options: It should be tested for electrical conductivity
It should be cleaned with a solvent
It should be inspected, gapped, and replaced
answers.answer: It should be inspected, gapped, and replaced
justification: But don’t go on to the next plug until you’ve read the plug (see the section “Reading your spark plugs” and Table 6-1) and gapped and replaced it, following the instructions later in this chapter.

answers.question: What does a large gap between the center and side electrodes likely indicate?
options: The center electrode has worn down
The spark plug is new and in perfect condition
The engine is running too cold
The spark plug is burning fuel/air mixture efficiently
answers.answer: The center electrode has worn down
justification:
If the gauge has a lot of room to wiggle around, it may be because your
old plug has worn down its center electrode, causing a gap that’s too
large.

answers.question: What part of the spark plug is referred to as the 'firing end'?
options: The end that was inside the cylinder
The terminal that connects to the spark plug socket
The threaded portion of the plug
The part that connects to the ignition coil
answers.answer: The end that was inside the cylinder
justification: Look at the plug’s firing end (the end that was inside the cylinder).

answers.question: What should you check for on the plug’s shell, insulator, and gaskets?
justification: Check the plug’s shell, insula-tor, and gaskets for signs of cracking or chipping, which indicate that the plug should be replaced.

answers.question: What is a probable cause of carbon-fouled spark plugs?
options: A fuel/air mixture that is too rich
answers.answer: A fuel/air mixture that is too rich
answers.justification:
Carbon-fouled Black, dry, fluffy soot 
Overly rich fuel/air 
mixture, dirty air

answers.question: What remedy is suggested for oil-fouled spark plugs?
options: Replace the plug and find the leak
justification: Oil-fouled plug Wet, black, oily deposits on insulator tip and electrodes Oil may be leaking into cylinders past worn pistons or poorly adjusted or worn valves Clean and regap the plug, or replace it, but find out where the leak is coming from.

answers.question: What characteristic suggests a spark plug is burned?
justification:
Burned plug
Blisters on insulator 
tip, melted electrodes, gap is too wide, 
wrong or loose 
plugs, overly lean 
fuel/air mixture, or 
incorrect timing

answers.question: What is the consequence of a center electrode wearing down?
options: The spark plug gap becomes too large
answers.answer: The spark plug gap becomes too large
justification: When the center electrode wears down, the gap becomes too large.

answers.question: What is recommended regarding new spark plugs?
options: They should be inspected for carbon buildup.
They should be scrubbed with a wire brush.
They should always be replaced immediately.
They do not need to be cleaned, but they do need to be gapped.
answers.answer: They do not need to be cleaned, but they do need to be gapped.
justification: Keep in mind that although you don’t need to clean new spark plugs, you do need to gap them.

answers.question: What is the recommended approach when replacing spark plugs to maintain engine efficiency?
options: Clean only the spark plugs that appear worn.
Replace only the worn spark plugs.
Mix new and old spark plugs to extend the lifespan of the old ones.
Replace all spark plugs with new ones or clean and reinstall all old ones.
answers.answer: Replace all spark plugs with new ones or clean and reinstall all old ones.
justification:
To keep your engine operating efficiently, don’t mix plugs in varying states of
wear. Either replace all the plugs with new ones or clean and reinstall all the old
ones.

answers.question: What is the significance of the number assigned to a spark plug?
options: It determines the plug's engine compatibility.
It denotes the plug's heat range; higher numbers indicate hotter plugs.
It signifies the plug's manufacturing date.
It indicates the spark plug's material composition.
answers.answer: It denotes the plug's heat range; higher numbers indicate hotter plugs.
justification: "You can identify these by the plug number: The higher the number, the hotter the plug."

answers.question: When dealing with spark plugs that exhibit varying degrees of wear, what course of action is suggested?
options: Clean and regap the usable plugs and store them for future emergencies.
Discard the plugs with the most wear.
Install the most worn plugs in the front of the engine.
Replace all plugs with the newest version available.
answers.answer: Clean and regap the usable plugs and store them for future emergencies.
justification: If you find that a few of your old plugs aren’t too worn and are in fairly good shape but you need to replace the others, clean and regap the salvageable plugs and store them in your trunk compartment tool kit for emergencies.

answers.question: What is the purpose of gapping spark plugs?
options: To adjust the distance between the electrodes for efficient firing.
To determine the spark plug's heat range.
To measure the spark plug's remaining lifespan.
To ensure the plugs are free from carbon buildup.
answers.answer: To adjust the distance between the electrodes for efficient firing.
justification: As I mention in the preceding section, the space, or gap, between the center and side electrodes needs to be an exact distance across; otherwise, your plugs don’t fire efficiently. Adjusting the distance between the two electrodes is called gapping your spark plugs.

answers.question: What should be done before regapping a used spark plug?
options: Apply a sealant to prevent corrosion.
Test the plug's ability to spark.
Clean the plug with a wire brush.
answers.answer: Clean the plug with a wire brush.
answers.justification: 1. If you’re regapping a used plug, make sure that it’s clean (gently scrub it with a wire brush).

answers.question: What should be done if a feeler gauge passes through the gap too easily without touching the electrodes?
options: The gap is too wide and needs to be narrowed.
The feeler gauge should be replaced.
The gap is too narrow and needs to be widened.
The electrodes need to be cleaned.
answers.answer: The gap is too wide and needs to be narrowed.
answers.justification: If the gauge goes through too easily without touching the electrodes, the gap is wide. Press the side electrode very gently against a clean, mar-proof surface until it’s slightly bent down toward the center electrode.

answers.question: When installing a spark plug, what is the recommended direction to wipe the spark plug hole?
justification: Wipe away from the hole; don’t shove any dirt into it.

answers.question: Where should oil be obtained to lightly coat the threads of the spark plug?
justification: Lightly coat the threads of the spark plug with a dab of oil from the oil dipstick, being careful not to get any on the center or side electrodes.

answers.question: In what direction should a spark plug be threaded when initially installing it?
options: It depends on the engine model.
justification: Carefully begin threading the spark plug into the engine by hand, turning it clockwise.

answers.question: What characteristic indicates the spark plug gap is properly adjusted?
options: The feeler gauge passes through the gap very easily.
The feeler gauge passes through the gap with difficulty, just catching the electrodes.
The feeler gauge does not pass through the gap.
answers.answer: The feeler gauge passes through the gap with difficulty, just catching the electrodes.
justification: You want the gauge to go through fairly easily, just catching the elec-\trodes as it passes.

answers.question: According to the instructions, how many turns should a spark plug be turned by hand before using a socket and ratchet?
options: One full turn B. Two full turns C. Three full turns D. Half a turn
answers.justification: 4. After you engage the plug by hand, turn it at least two full turns before utilizing the spark plug socket and ratchet.

answers.question: What is a potential consequence of over-tightening a spark plug?
options: Increased fuel efficiency B. Cracking the porcelain C. Extending the spark plug's lifespan D. Reducing engine noise
justification: Don’t over-tighten the plug (you can crack the porcelain); just get it in nice and tight with no wiggle.

answers.question: What characteristic of a spark plug cable might indicate the need for replacement?
options: A shiny exterior B. A uniform color C. Cracks, brittleness, or fraying D. A perfectly smooth surface
justification:
If you have trouble holding onto the plug, you can buy a spark plug
starter and fit it over the plug. Or, you can use just about anything you
can wrap around or slip over the plug top, including an old spark plug
wire boot, a finger cut from a vinyl glove, an old piece of thin plastic
tubing, or a piece of vacuum hose.
4. After you engage the plug by hand, turn it at least two full turns
before utilizing the spark plug socket and ratchet.
5. Slip the spark plug socket over the spark plug, attach the ratchet handle,
and continue turning the plug clockwise until you meet resistance. Don’t
overtighten the plug (you can crack the porcelain); just get it in
nice and tight with no wiggle. The plug should stick a little when you try
to loosen it, but you should be able to loosen it again without straining
yourself. Tighten and loosen the first plug once or twice to get the
proper feel of the thing.
6. Take a look at the spark plug cable before attaching its boot to the
plug. If the cable appears cracked, brittle, or frayed or is saturated
with oil, have it replaced.

answers.question: What is the recommended action after completing a task on a vehicle's electrical system?
options: Wash your hands with hand cleaner.
answers.answer: Wash your hands with hand cleaner.
justification: When you’re done, start your engine to prove to yourself that everything still\nworks. Then wash your hands with hand cleaner.

answers.question: What is the purpose of recording a battery's purchase and expected lifespan?
options: To assist with monthly under-the-hood checks.
To prevent being stranded with a dead battery.
To extend the battery's life expectancy.
To determine when to jump-start the vehicle.
answers.answer: To prevent being stranded with a dead battery.
justification: To prevent being stuck on the road with a dead battery, enter that information in your Specifications Record in Appendix B and have the battery replaced before it comes to the end of its life expectancy.

answers.question: According to the material, which chapter provides instructions for jump-starting a vehicle?
justification: And if it has just run out of juice temporarily because you left the lights on, Chapter 21 tells you all about jumping a start.

answers.question: What is the primary reason to consider having a battery professionally installed?
options: Because it is easier than replacing it yourself.
To prevent damage to other vehicle components.
If installation and disposal costs are included in the new battery price.
answers.answer: If installation and disposal costs are included in the new battery price.
justification: However, if installation and disposal are included in the price of a new battery, there may be no advan- tage in undertaking the job.

answers.question: If a battery is concealed under a plastic shield, what should be assessed before attempting replacement?
options: The overall condition of the engine.
The complexity of the shield's attachment.
answers.answer: The complexity of the shield's attachment.
justification: If the battery on your vehicle is concealed under a plastic shield, take a look at what’s holding it in place. If you see just a few screws or bolts, you can probably unscrew them and remove the shield without much trouble. If it looks diffi-cult to get the thing off, have the battery installed wher-ever you buy it.

answers.question: Where should you find specifications for the battery designed for your vehicle?
options: An inexpensive pair of safety goggles
justification: If you haven’t already put the information on your Specifications Record, consult your owner’s manual to find the specifications for the battery designed for your vehicle.

answers.question: Batteries are typically priced based on what factor?
justification: Batteries are priced by their life expectancy.

answers.question: What is the primary purpose of wearing disposable latex gloves during a battery replacement?
options: To prevent skin contact with battery deposits
answers.answer: To prevent skin contact with battery deposits
justification: To protect your eyes from any deposits that can hurt them, invest in an inexpensive pair of safety goggles, too.

answers.question: What should you do if the new battery you purchased does not match the original battery's size, shape, or configuration?
options: Return the battery and obtain the correct one.
answers.answer: Return the battery and obtain the correct one.
justification: If it isn’t, march right back in and return it for the right one.

answers.question: What is the purpose of placing a blanket or pad over the fender when replacing a battery?
options: To act as a cushion for tools.
To prevent the vehicle from moving.
To protect the battery from damage.
To protect the fender from scratches.
answers.answer: To protect the fender from scratches.
justification:
To protect your eyes from any deposits that can hurt them, invest in an inexpensive pair of safety goggles, too.
How to remove and install a battery
When you have your equipment handy and you’re ready to start, follow these
easy steps:
1. Make sure that your vehicle is in Park, with the engine shut off and
the parking brake on.
2. Open the hood and place a blanket or pad over the fender to protect it.

answers.question: If a bolt is difficult to loosen, what is a recommended technique?
options: Using two wrenches and moving them in opposite directions
Using a single wrench and applying more force
justification:
If you have trouble loosening the bolt, grab it with one wrench and the
 nut with another, and move the wrenches in opposite directions.

answers.question: What is the primary reason to avoid discarding old batteries in regular trash?
options: They contain a toxic, corrosive liquid
They can be reused for other purposes
answers.answer: They contain a toxic, corrosive liquid
justification: Batteries are filled with a toxic, corrosive liquid and must be disposed of properly.

answers.question: What is a potential remedy for a rusty battery tray?
options: Cleaning with baking soda dissolved in water
answers.answer: Cleaning with baking soda dissolved in water
justification: 6. If the tray on which the battery was standing is rusty or has deposits\n on it, clean it with a little baking soda dissolved in water.

answers.question: What is the usual order for replacing battery cables if a vehicle has a negative ground?
options: It doesn't matter which cable is replaced first
The positive cable first, then the negative cable
The negative cable first, then the positive cable
answers.answer: The positive cable first, then the negative cable
justification: (If your vehicle has negative ground, the positive cable goes back first.)

answers.question: What is a common cause of malfunctions in vehicle electronics such as stereos or turn signals?
answers.justification:
If your stereo goes dead, your turn signals don’t blink, a light goes out, or
 some other gadget stops working, it’s often just the result of a blown fuse.

answers.question: What is one reason to locate fuse boxes?
options: To familiarize yourself with the vehicle's design.
To improve the vehicle's fuel efficiency.
To avoid unnecessary equipment replacement or repairs.
answers.answer: To avoid unnecessary equipment replacement or repairs.
justification: than paying for new equipment or repairs that you don’t need (even if you

answers.question: Prior to working on a fuse box, what safety measure should be taken?
options: Check the owner's manual for troubleshooting tips.
justification:
Before you open or work on a fuse box, be sure that your vehicle’s ignition is
        turned off.

answers.question: Where can you typically find information regarding the location of fuse boxes?
justification: Your owner’s manual can help you locate them.

answers.question: If a headlight fails to operate on the low beam setting but functions correctly on the high beam setting, what is the likely solution?
answers.justification: If a headlight doesn’t work on Low beam but does work on High, you have to replace the whole sealed-beam unit or headlamp.

answers.question: When both headlights simultaneously cease to function, what is the most probable cause?
options: A problem with the sealed-beam units
justification: If both of your headlights go out at the same time, chances are the units are okay but the fuse that controls them has blown and needs to be changed.

answers.question: What is one way to determine the type of headlights your vehicle has?
options: Observing their appearance when illuminated at night
answers.answer: Observing their appearance when illuminated at night
answers.justification: You can tell which type of headlights you have by looking at them when they’re on at night.

answers.question: If a headlight is not working in either the high or low beam settings, what is the most likely cause?
options: A bad connection in the wiring
answers.answer: A bad connection in the wiring
answers.justification: If a headlight doesn’t work on either High or Low beam, you probably just have a bad connection in the wiring.

answers.question: What is one possible reason why directional signals might not flash in synchronicity?
answers.answer: The signal lights themselves may not be malfunctioning.
justification: If any of your directional signals stop flashing or don’t flash in synch, or if the directional signal indicators on your dashboard don’t flash, the signal lights themselves may not be malfunctioning.

answers.question: Older vehicles often require ignition tune-ups annually.
justification: unlike older vehicles, which needed ignition tune-ups at least once a year, most vehicles on the road today have electronic ignition systems that are built to go for years without the need for them

answers.question: Major tune-ups typically require specialized equipment.
justification: because major tune-ups require electronic diagnostic equip-ment and sensitive onboard computers, they should be done by profession-als at the dealership or a qualified service facility

answers.question: It is always advisable to perform all electrical system maintenance yourself.
justification: Although this chapter provides instructions for quite a number of jobs you can do yourself, because major tune-ups require electronic diagnostic equip-ment and sensitive onboard computers, they should be done by professionals at the dealership or a qualified service facility.

answers.question: Replacing air and fuel filters can be part of a tune-up.
justification: In case you’re wondering what a modern tune-up includes, it generally involves doing an electronic diagnostic check, changing spark plugs, replacing air and fuel filters, and checking fluid levels.

answers.question: Newer vehicles with electronic ignition and fuel injection systems typically require more frequent tune-ups than older vehicles.
justification: Most older vehicles with non-electronic ignitions should be tuned every 10,000 to 12,000 miles or every year, whichever comes first. Newer cars with electronic ignition and fuel injection systems are scheduled to go from 25,000 miles to as many as 100,000 miles without needing a major tune-up.

answers.question: Refrigerant escaping from an air conditioning system can be harmful.
justification: It contains refrigerant under pressure that can blind you if it escapes.

answers.question: A vehicle stalling only when the air conditioner is on provides useful information for troubleshooting.
justification:
If you’re having trouble pinpointing why your vehicle is stalling, you can
help your automotive technician diagnose the problem by paying atten-
ion to whether the engine stalls when it’s hot, cold, or when the air 
conditioner is on.

answers.question: A vehicle’s spark plugs always need to be replaced according to a strict mileage schedule.
justification: How often you replace spark plugs depends on the type of plugs you have...Although some professionals recommend replacement every 60,000 miles to avoid damage to the engine.

answers.question: Spark plug wires can be identified by looking for thick wires or thin cables entering the engine block.
answers.justification: To locate your spark plugs, look for a set of thick wires (or thin cables) that enter your engine block in neat rows...

answers.question: Modern vehicles often have engine compartments that make spark plug replacement straightforward.
justification: Because so many modern vehicles have crammed engine compartments that make access diffi-cult

answers.question: It is safe to proceed with spark plug replacement on any vehicle, provided you have the correct tools.
justification: If I were you, I’d leave all these vehicles to the professionals who have hoists and special tools that allow them to do the work safely and with relative ease.

answers.question: Replacing a spark plug on a vehicle with an aluminum cylinder head carries a lower risk of damage compared to those with steel heads.
justification: Your engine has an aluminum cylinder head. Because the metal is soft, it’s easy to strip the threads in the spark plug’s hole. If you do, the head will probably have to be removed for repair, and that means more mega-bucks.

answers.question: If spark plugs are hidden, it might require removing other components to access them.
justification: On some vehicles, you can’t get at the plugs until you remove other parts that are in the way. For example, on some transverse engines, you’d have to remove the top engine mount bolts in order to tilt the engine forward to replace the rear spark plugs. And on other engines, you can only get to some spark plugs from underneath or through the wheel well area.

answers.question: Ignoring the accessibility of spark plugs can lead to problems like increased fuel consumption.
justification:
Studies have found that many shops just don’t bother to dismount the
engine to get at difficult plugs. If left unchanged, that one funky plug can
increase your fuel consumption, add to air pollution, and cause other
problems.

answers.question: Older domestic engines are generally less likely to have systems that place an ignition coil directly atop each spark plug.
justification: Some older domestic engines, most modern import engines, and an increasing number of modern domestic engines have systems that place an ignition coil directly atop each spark plug.

answers.question: It is acceptable to change only a few spark plugs to save money.
justification: Never change just a few plugs; it’s all or nothing for even engine performance.

answers.question: Spark plugs can become stale over time and should be used quickly.
justification: Spark plugs don’t get stale.

answers.question: A work light or flashlight is necessary for this procedure.
justification: A work light (or flashlight, at least)

answers.question: The spark plugs should be installed without any lubricant.
justification: The threads of the spark plugs should be lightly coated with a dab of engine oil off the oil dipstick or with anti-seize lubricant before you install them in the engine.

answers.question: A vehicle's owner’s manual is the only place to find spark plug specifications.
justification: Your owner’s manual may have specifications for buying and gapping the spark plugs on your vehicle. If you don’t have an owner’s manual, or if yours lacks the necessary information, you can find the correct spark plugs and spark plug gap in a general “Tune-Up Specification Guide”.

answers.question: The type of transmission (automatic or manual) is irrelevant when determining spark plug specifications.
justification: Whether the vehicle has an automatic or a manual (standard) transmission.

answers.question: Air conditioning is a crucial factor to consider when purchasing most automotive parts.
justification: It’s not usually necessary to take this into account when buying most parts, but you never know when you’ll need it.

answers.question: The number of barrels in a carburetor is not a distinguishing factor for older engines.
justification: Carburetors, on the other hand, were distinguished by how many “barrels” they had.

answers.question: Engine displacement directly correlates to the amount of fuel and air that cylinders hold.
justification: The bigger the displace- ment, the more fuel and air the cylinders in the engine hold.

answers.question: It is advisable to remove all spark plugs at once to speed up the replacement process.
justification:
One way to royally confuse yourself and turn the relatively simple task of
changing spark plugs into a nightmare is to pull all your spark plugs out
 at one time. To keep your sanity and to avoid turning this job into an all-\ weekend project, work on one spark plug at a time: Remove it, inspect it, clean it, and — if it’s salvageable — gap it. Then replace it before you move on
to the next spark plug in cylinder sequence order.

answers.question: The material suggests that it is always better to attempt a repair yourself, regardless of your skill level.
justification: “Deciding if you should do the job yourself,” and if your vehicle fits even one of those descriptions, don’t try to do the work in this section. It’s better to pay a professional than to risk damaging your engine or ECU!”

answers.question: Maintaining the correct firing order is important for proper engine function.
justification: To maintain the proper firing order, each spark-plug wire must go from the spark source to the proper spark plug.

answers.question: Universal extensions can allow the ratchet handle to be held at unusual angles.
justification: There are universal extensions that allow the ratchet handle to be held at odd angles

answers.question: A service station will be delighted to change just one spark plug.
justification: If you absolutely can’t reach the offending plug, you can always drive to your service station and humbly ask them to change just that one plug. They won’t like it, but it is a last resort.

answers.question: It is recommended to apply maximum force to loosen a stuck spark plug.
justification: It’s okay to exert some strength; just be sure your socket is securely over the plug...

answers.question: The ratchet handle allows for precise control over the direction of socket rotation.
justification: The little knob on the back of the ratchet handle causes the ratchet to turn the socket either clockwise or counterclockwise.
If your ratchet clicks in the wrong direction, just move that little knob to reverse the direction.

answers.question: If the ratchet clicks in the wrong direction, it is necessary to replace the ratchet.
justification: If your ratchet clicks in the wrong direction, just move that little knob to reverse the direction.

answers.question: Once the ratchet turns freely, it’s best to continue using the ratchet handle to remove the plug.
justification: 6. When the ratchet turns freely, finish the job by removing the ratchet handle and turning the socket by hand until the plug is free from the engine.

answers.question: Consistent maintenance can make it easier to remove spark plugs in the future.
justification: You’ll feel better knowing that after you’ve installed your new plugs by hand, it will be a lot easier to get them loose the next time.

answers.question: It’s acceptable to skip reading a spark plug and immediately proceed with replacing it.
justification: But don’t go on to the next plug until you’ve read the plug (see the section “Reading your spark plugs” and Table 6-1) and gapped and replaced it, following the instructions later in this chapter.

answers.question: A smaller-than-specified gap between the electrodes improves engine efficiency.
justification: If the gauge can’t fit between the center and side electrodes, the gap is too small, which means that the spark plug isn’t burning the fuel/air mixture efficiently.

answers.question: The center electrode and side electrode are responsible for creating the spark.
justification: The spark comes up the center of the plug and jumps the gap between these two electrodes.

answers.question: New spark plugs always require cleaning before installation.
justification: Keep in mind that although you don’t need to clean new spark plugs, you do need to gap them.

answers.question: A burned spark plug is likely caused by a lean fuel/air mixture.
justification:
Burned plug
Blisters on insulator 
tip, melted electrodes, gap is too wide, 
wrong or loose 
plugs, overly lean 
fuel/air mixture, or 
incorrect timing

answers.question: It is unnecessary to check the gap of spark plugs that are sold “pre-gapped.”
justification: Keep in mind that although you don’t need to clean new spark plugs, you do need to gap them. Some plugs are sold “pre-gapped,” but I recom- mend checking them with a feeler gauge anyway.

answers.question: A worn spark plug will maintain the original spark plug gap.
justification: When the center electrode wears down, the gap becomes too large.

answers.question: Carbon fouling is often a result of driving at high speeds.
answers.justification: Carbon-fouled Black, dry, fluffy soot on insulator tip and electrodes Overly rich fuel/air mixture, dirty air filter, too much driving at low speeds, or idling for a long time

answers.question: It is unnecessary to gap new spark plugs, even if the package indicates they are 'pre-gapped'.
justification: Gap new as well as old spark plugs, even if the package says that the new plugs are “pre-gapped.”

answers.question: Adjusting spark plug heat range should not exceed one step hotter or cooler at a time.
justification: Never go more than one step hotter or cooler at a time.

answers.question: Checking for cracks or bubbles in the porcelain insulator is a recommended inspection step.
justification: You shouldn’t see any cracks or bubbles in the porcelain insulator

answers.question: It is uncommon to experience adjustments that oscillate between a gap that is too large and too small.
justification: If you keep adjusting the gap too narrow or too wide, don’t feel bad. Everyone I know goes through the “too large–too small–too large” bit a couple of times for each plug, especially the perfectionists.

answers.question: Oil should be applied to both the center and side electrodes of the spark plug during installation.
justification: being careful not to get any on the center or side electrodes.

answers.question: The initial threading of the spark plug should be done with a wrench.
justification: Carefully begin threading the spark plug into the engine by hand, turning it clockwise.

answers.question: Wiping away from the spark plug hole prevents dirt from entering the cylinder.
justification:
1. Clean the spark plug hole in the cylinder block with a clean, lint-free
cloth.
Wipe away from the hole; don’t shove any dirt into it.

answers.question: It is safe to use any tool to hold a spark plug during installation as long as it fits.
justification: Or, you can use just about anything you can wrap around or slip over the plug top, including an old spark plug wire boot, a finger cut from a vinyl glove, an old piece of thin plastic tubing, or a piece of vacuum hose.

answers.question: A spark plug should be tightened so that it can easily be loosened with hand pressure.
justification: The plug should stick a little when you try to loosen it, but you should be able to loosen it again without straining yourself.

answers.question: Cars with fewer cylinders generally require less time and effort to service spark plugs.
justification: It’s at times like these that owners of 4-cylinder cars have the edge on those who drive those big, expensive 8-cylinder monsters.

answers.question: It is always advantageous to replace a battery yourself, regardless of the cost or difficulty.
justification: However, if installation and disposal are included in the price of a new battery, there may be no advantage in undertaking the job.

answers.question: Regular battery checks and maintenance can help extend its lifespan.
justification: To extend its life, your battery should be checked and maintained regularly.

answers.question: A vehicle's Specifications Record should be consulted to track battery information.
justification: To prevent being stuck on the road with a dead battery, enter that information in your Specifications Record in Appendix B and have the battery replaced before it comes to the end of its life expectancy.

answers.question: If a battery is easily accessible and has a few screws or bolts securing its shield, it is likely safe to replace yourself.
answers.justification: If you see just a few screws or bolts, you can probably unscrew them and remove the shield without much trouble.

answers.question: It is advisable to purchase the most expensive battery regardless of how long you plan to keep your vehicle.
justification: but if you don’t plan to keep your vehicle longer than five years, don’t spring for an expensive long-term battery that will vastly outlive your need for it.

answers.question: Wearing safety goggles is unnecessary when working with batteries.
justification: To protect your eyes from any deposits that can hurt them, invest in an inexpensive pair of safety goggles, too.

answers.question: If your vehicle has positive ground, you should loosen the cable on the negative terminal first.
justification: If your vehicle has positive ground, loosen the cable with “+” or “POS” on it first.

answers.question: It is recommended to replace battery cables in the same order they were originally installed.
justification: 9. Replace the battery cables on the terminals in reverse order from which you removed them.

answers.question: Replacing a blown fuse is typically an expensive repair.
justification: You can change fuses yourself, easily and with very little expense.

answers.question: Wearing gloves is recommended when handling a battery due to potentially corrosive materials.
justification: Wear your gloves because the battery stuff is corrosive and be sure the battery tray is completely dry before taking the next step!

answers.question: Fuse boxes are difficult to recognize due to their complex design.
justification: A fuse box is easy to recognize (see Figures 6-6 and 6-7)

answers.question: Shops will always recycle old batteries for free when a new battery is purchased.
justification: If you have your new battery installed when you buy it, the shop will recycle the old one for you. They’ll probably want to charge a few dollars for this service, but try to negotiate it into the price.

answers.question: It is recommended to avoid working on fuse boxes to prevent potential mistakes.
justification:
Before you open or work on a fuse box, be sure that your vehicle’s ignition is
 turned off.

answers.question: The owner’s manual can assist in locating fuse boxes.
justification: Your owner’s manual can help you locate them.

answers.question: Working on a fuse box is only safe if a professional mechanic is present.
justification: Before you open or work on a fuse box, be sure that your vehicle’s ignition is turned off.

answers.question: If a headlight fails on both the low and high beam settings, the headlight unit itself is always the problem.
justification: If a headlight doesn’t work on either High or Low beam, you probably just have a bad connection in the wiring.

answers.question: Washing headlights and taillights periodically can help improve nighttime visibility.
justification: It doesn’t hurt to wash the outside of your headlights and taillights occasion-ally; clean lights provide better visibility at night.

answers.question: Sealed-beam headlights are typically found in newer vehicles.
justification: Older vehicles have headlights with sealed-beam units (bulbs). Modern vehicles have headlamps filled with halogen or Xenon gas.

answers.question: A blown fuse can be the reason for both headlights failing simultaneously.
answers.justification: If both of your headlights go out at the same time, chances are the units are okay but the fuse that controls them has blown and needs to be changed.

answers.question: Determining the headlight type is unnecessary before attempting replacement.
justification: Before attempting to replace or adjust your headlights, you need to know whether you have halogen or Xenon headlamps or the old-style sealed-beam units.

answers.question: Xenon lamps consume the most power of the headlight types discussed.
justification: Xenon are the brightest, have the longest life, and consume the least power.

answers.question: Sealed-beam headlights offer more design flexibility compared to modern headlights.
justification: Halogen and Xenon headlamps have also allowed designers to get pretty creative with shapes because they use small, replaceable lamps that don’t have to be contained in round or rectangular housings.

answers.question: It is acceptable to use any tool available to remove a headlight's exterior rings or frames.
justification: If you need some exotic tool to do this, forget the job and have it done professionally.

answers.question: Handling a replacement bulb by its plastic base is a recommended practice.
justification: Instead, handle the bulb by its plastic base or the metallic tip, if it has one.

answers.question: The primary purpose of adjusting headlight alignment is to improve vehicle aesthetics.
justification:
Because HID headlamps are so
in tensely bright, it’s most important
to avoid temporarily blinding other
drivers, pedestrians, or animals.

answers.question: It is safe to apply excessive force to loosen stuck retaining screws.
justification: If the retaining screws stick, spray a little penetrating solvent (like WD-40) on them.

answers.question: Rectangular headlights have fewer screws than round headlights.
justification:
The plate has several screws; round headlights have three screws that
loosen the plate, and rectangular headlights have four.

answers.question: Professional headlight alignment is always free.
justification: Note, though, that if you go to a repair facility that does MVB inspections just to see whether your headlights are aimed properly, you’ll probably have to pay them for checking the lights and for an inspection certificate whether or not they have to adjust your lights.

answers.question: The wiring connector should be checked for corrosion and wear.
justification: Scrape any corrosion off the connector, and check its wiring for wear.

answers.question: Aligning headlights according to state laws can often be done at Motor Vehicle Bureau inspection stations.
justification: Some are certified Motor Vehicle Bureau inspection stations.

answers.question: A certificate verifying headlight adjustment is typically included in the cost of the check.
justification: This certificate is usually part of the price.

answers.question: Troubleshooting information for directional signals can be found in Chapter 6.
justification: You can find information about troubleshooting directional signals in Chapter 20.
